operations.value: Rare cases and case series of clinically significant liver injury have been associated with ashwagandha products, often with cholestatic or mixed patterns; discontinue use and seek medical evaluation if jaundice, dark urine, marked itching, or persistent upper-abdominal symptoms develop.
operations.notes: The warning is based on case-series and causality-assessment literature. It does not imply that liver injury is common.

sources.title: Ashwagandha-induced liver injury—A case series from India and literature review
Herb-Induced Liver Injury by Ayurvedic Ashwagandha as Assessed for Causality by the Updated RUCAM: An Emerging Cause

notes: Adds a rare-but-important liver safety warning with symptom-based escalation guidance.
